howItWorks.intro: This cholesterol ratio calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Health metrics are general guidelines. Consult your healthcare provider for personalized medical advice.

faq.question: What is a good Total/HDL ratio?
faq.answer: Below 3.5 is ideal. 3.5-5.0 is acceptable. Above 5.0 indicates elevated cardiovascular risk. This ratio is often more predictive than total cholesterol alone.

faq.question: Why does the Trig/HDL ratio matter?
faq.answer: Trig/HDL under 2.0 suggests healthy insulin sensitivity. Over 3.0 may indicate insulin resistance and metabolic syndrome, even with otherwise normal cholesterol.

faq.question: How do I improve my ratios?
faq.answer: Raise HDL: exercise, omega-3s, moderate alcohol. Lower LDL: reduce saturated fat, eat fiber, consider statins. Lower triglycerides: reduce sugar, alcohol, and refined carbs.

faq.question: How often should I test?
faq.answer: Every 4-6 years for adults with normal levels. Annually if levels are abnormal or you have risk factors. More often if on cholesterol medication.

faq.question: Is the Cholesterol Ratio Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Cholesterol Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Cholesterol Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your total cholesterol, hdl, ldl, you get instant results including total/hdl ratio, ldl/hdl ratio, trig/hdl ratio. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total/hdl ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your total cholesterol and need to find the right total/hdl ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your total cholesterol — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: hdl, ldl, triglycerides. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total/hdl ratio, ldl/hdl ratio, trig/hdl ratio. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your total cholesterol before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total/hdl ratio and ldl/hdl ratio — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your triglycerides, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
